quiz.title: SAT: Unit 2 Quiz 6
quiz.subtitle: Connecting Passage Claims with Quantitative Information (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: SAT Preparation

questions.question:
Ecologists claim that invasive plant species reduce native biodiversity primarily by outcompeting natives for soil nutrients, not by altering habitat structure.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|}\hline\text{Plot Type} & \text{Native Species Count} & \text{Soil Nitrogen (ppm)} \\ \hline\text{Control (No Invasive)} & 24 & 18 \\ \hline\text{Low Invasive Density} & 19 & 12 \\ \hline\text{High Invasive Density} & 8 & 4 \\ \hline\end{array}$$

Which choice best describes how the data relates to the ecologists' claim?
options: Habitat-structure surveys indicate greater spatial heterogeneity in high-density invasive plots.
Nutrient depletion accompanies invasive expansion alongside native-species reductions.
Soil nitrogen concentrations show minimal fluctuation across experimental plot conditions.
Native-species recovery follows invasive-plant removal interventions in controlled settings.
questions.explanation: Choice B neutrally describes the parallel decline in both variables, which students must then evaluate against the nutrient-competition claim. Choice A introduces habitat structure, a variable not measured in the table. Choice C contradicts the visible nitrogen decline. Choice D describes an untested intervention (removal) not present in the data.

questions.question:
Economists propose that remote work adoption reduces urban commercial real estate demand but increases suburban residential property values.

Which finding from the market analysis best supports this proposal?
options: Commercial vacancy metrics and suburban home valuations moved in opposite directions post-policy shift.
Property tax assessments declined across three metropolitan areas following remote-work adoption.
Transit infrastructure planning expanded in suburban jurisdictions accommodating commuter redistribution.
Home-office equipment expenditures averaged twenty-four hundred dollars annually per remote worker.
questions.explanation: Choice A directly evidences both predicted outcomes: rising urban commercial vacancy and increasing suburban residential values. Choice B addresses only one side (commercial) and focuses on taxes, not demand. Choice C discusses transit infrastructure, not property values. Choice D examines worker spending, not real estate market trends.

questions.question:
A cognitive scientist hypothesizes that multitasking impairs memory encoding not by reducing attention span, but by interfering with the consolidation of information into long-term storage.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|}\hline\text{Condition} & \text{Immediate Recall (%)} & \text{Delayed Recall (%)} \\ \hline\text{Single-Task} & 84 & 79 \\ \hline\text{Dual-Task} & 81 & 52 \\ \hline\text{Triple-Task} & 78 & 31 \\ \hline\end{array}$$

Which choice best describes how the data relates to the hypothesis?
options: Attention-assessment batteries yielded comparable scores across single-, dual-, and triple-task conditions.
Post-experiment fatigue ratings increased proportionally with the number of concurrent task demands.
Delayed-recall performance deteriorates markedly as concurrent task load escalates beyond single-task baselines.
Inter-task rest intervals facilitate memory consolidation regardless of initial encoding complexity.
questions.explanation: Choice C neutrally reports the divergent trends: small immediate-recall changes vs. large delayed-recall declines, which students must evaluate against the consolidation-interference claim. Choice A introduces unmeasured attention-span data. Choice B cites subjective fatigue, not memory performance. Choice D describes an untested intervention (rest periods).

questions.question:
Public health researchers claim that community vaccination campaigns achieve herd immunity faster when targeting high-transmission age groups first, rather than distributing doses evenly across all demographics.

Which outcome from the campaign evaluation best supports this claim?
options: Immunogenicity profiles remained consistent across demographic categories regardless of vaccination sequencing.
Community outreach initiatives boosted public confidence in vaccination programs within underserved neighborhoods.
Mobile clinic deployment reduced per-dose administrative costs relative to fixed-site vaccination facilities.
Targeted adolescent vaccination achieved population-level immunity thresholds with substantially lower coverage requirements.
questions.explanation: Choice D directly supports the strategic-targeting claim by showing lower overall coverage achieved herd immunity faster when high-transmission groups were prioritized. Choice A addresses vaccine efficacy, not distribution strategy. Choice B discusses public trust, not transmission dynamics. Choice C focuses on logistical costs, not epidemiological outcomes.

questions.question:
Linguists propose that language change accelerates in communities with high rates of adult second-language acquisition because learners simplify complex grammatical structures, which then spread to native speakers.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|}\hline\text{Community} & \text{Adult L2 Learners (%)} & \text{Grammatical Simplification Index} \\ \hline\text{Alpha} & 12 & 2.1 \\ \hline\text{Beta} & 38 & 4.7 \\ \hline\text{Gamma} & 61 & 7.9 \\ \hline\end{array}$$

Which choice best describes how the data relates to the linguists' proposal?
options: Lexical innovation rates accelerate in immigrant-dense communities without corresponding syntactic simplification.
Simplification indices track closely with adult second-language learner proportions across surveyed communities.
Phonological acquisition outpaces morphological mastery among adult learners regardless of native language background.
Case-system complexity inversely predicts adult acquisition speed relative to fixed-word-order language structures.
questions.explanation: Choice B neutrally describes the positive correlation between L2 learner percentage and simplification index, which students must evaluate against the proposed transmission mechanism. Choice A addresses lexical change, not grammatical simplification. Choice C describes acquisition patterns without linking them to community-level change. Choice D compares language features without addressing the spread of simplifications.

questions.question:
A marine biologist claims that coral bleaching events are driven more by prolonged temperature anomalies than by short-term heat spikes.

Which finding from the study best supports this claim?
options: Extended moderate warming produces substantially higher bleaching prevalence than brief extreme thermal events.
Regional temperature variability spanned one to five degrees Celsius above seasonal baselines during monitoring.
Thicker-tissued coral species demonstrate enhanced thermal tolerance across diverse temperature exposure regimes.
Solar radiation intensity correlates with bleaching severity independent of water temperature fluctuations.
questions.explanation: Choice A directly contrasts prolonged moderate anomalies with brief extreme spikes, supporting the claim about duration over intensity. Choice B describes temperature ranges without linking them to bleaching outcomes. Choice C introduces a biological variable (tissue thickness) unrelated to the duration claim. Choice D shifts focus to light intensity, a confounding factor not central to the hypothesis.

questions.question:
Educational researchers argue that handwriting lecture notes improves long-term memory retention by forcing students to synthesize information rather than transcribe it verbatim.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|}\hline\text{Note-Taking Method} & \text{Verbatim Recall (%)} & \text{Conceptual Recall (%)} \\ \hline\text{Handwritten} & 41 & 68 \\ \hline\text{Typed} & 73 & 49 \\ \hline\text{Audio Recording} & 38 & 33 \\ \hline\end{array}$$

Which choice best describes how the data relates to the researchers' argument?
options: Audio-recording methods produce the lowest performance outcomes across both recall measurement categories.
Typing speed advantages facilitate higher verbatim transcription accuracy relative to manual handwriting.
Conceptual understanding benefits from handwritten note-taking despite reduced verbatim transcription fidelity.
Disciplinary preferences influence note-taking method selection across humanities and STEM course contexts.
questions.explanation: Choice C neutrally reports the trade-off pattern: handwritten notes sacrifice verbatim accuracy for conceptual understanding, which students must evaluate against the synthesis claim. Choice A describes audio recording performance but doesn't address the handwritten-vs-typed comparison central to the argument. Choice B introduces typing speed, an unmeasured variable. Choice D cites course preferences, not memory outcomes.

questions.question:
Urban planners hypothesize that expanding municipal green spaces reduces resident stress levels by providing accessible environments for physiological recovery.

Which finding would provide the most relevant evidence for this hypothesis?
options: Residential property valuations increase following municipal landscaping investments in adjacent neighborhoods.
Urban tree canopies sequester approximately forty-eight pounds of atmospheric carbon dioxide annually per specimen.
Individual self-reports indicate subjective relaxation following botanical garden exposure among community members.
Physiological stress markers show measurable reduction among residents proximate to municipal green space access.
questions.explanation: Choice D provides direct physiological data linking proximity to green spaces with reduced stress markers, directly testing the hypothesized mechanism. Choice A addresses economic impact, not stress physiology. Choice B discusses environmental benefits unrelated to human stress responses. Choice C relies on a single anecdote rather than systematic evidence.

questions.question:
A historian argues that the construction of transcontinental railroads in the 19th century accelerated economic integration more by standardizing time zones than by reducing transport costs.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|}\hline\text{Metric} & \text{1870} & \text{1890} \\ \hline\text{Avg. Freight Cost (per ton-mile)} & 2.8\textcent & 1.7\textcent \\ \hline\text{Municipalities on Standard Time} & 12 & 318 \\ \hline\text{Interstate Commerce Volume} & \$42\text{M} & \$217\text{M} \\ \hline\end{array}$$

Which choice best describes how the data relates to the historian's argument?
options: Freight-cost reductions demonstrate strong correlation with interstate commerce expansion across regional markets.
Early standard-time adopters experienced accelerated economic development relative to delayed-implementation municipalities.
Transport-cost declines preceded widespread time-zone standardization adoption by approximately five-year intervals.
Time-standardization adoption expanded dramatically while freight-cost reductions remained comparatively modest.
questions.explanation: Choice D neutrally compares the magnitude of change in the two variables central to the argument, which students must evaluate against the claim that time-zone standardization had greater impact. Choice A focuses only on freight costs, ignoring the time-zone variable. Choice B introduces economic growth, an outcome not directly measured in the table. Choice C makes a temporal claim about sequencing not verifiable from the two time points.

questions.question:
Nutritionists claim that replacing red meat with plant-based proteins reduces systemic inflammation by lowering saturated fat intake and increasing antioxidant consumption.

Which finding from the clinical trial best supports this claim?
options: Livestock production systems contribute substantially to atmospheric methane emissions accelerating climate processes.
Plant-based grocery items frequently command premium pricing relative to conventional meat products in retail markets.
Clinical biomarker assessments indicate significant inflammatory-marker reductions following legume-based dietary substitution.
Consumer taste preferences often favor traditional beef preparations over lentil-based alternative protein sources.
questions.explanation: Choice C provides clinical biomarker data directly measuring the claimed physiological mechanism (reduced inflammation). Choice A addresses environmental impact, not human health outcomes. Choice B discusses market pricing, irrelevant to biological effects. Choice D cites personal taste preferences, not scientific evidence.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent work! You can accurately connect textual claims with quantitative evidence and evaluate whether data supports specific mechanisms.
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review missed questions and focus on whether the data directly addresses the mechanism or outcome stated in the claim.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Remember: factual accuracy is not enough. Evidence must be directly relevant to the specific argument being made.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! Ask yourself: does this data measure the exact variable or relationship the claim describes?
